title: Selected attention/KV NoC scheduler refinement
hypothesis: The capacity-constrained attention/KV frontier should be re-ranked at a few transition points once SRAM bank pressure, NoC hop latency, arbitration efficiency, virtual-channel relief, and producer overlap bounds are modeled explicitly.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Do the practical die-size transition points chosen by the capacity/NoC baseline remain attractive when selected-point scheduling pressure is included?
include: gpt2_small at 131k tokens around the 100 to 200 mm2 transition
gpt2_medium_proxy at 131k tokens around the 200 to 400 mm2 transition
llama7b_proxy at 131k tokens in the 400 mm2 spill-heavy case
strict serialized latency and an overlap lower bound
exclude: router placement and routed wire delay

expected_benefit: separates capacity feasibility from scheduling feasibility
identifies whether selected transition points are memory, NoC, producer, or compute limited
chooses the next detailed memory/NoC RTL or scheduler target without broadening the sweep further
risks: the overlap bound is optimistic and should not be treated as a final latency
bank conflicts are modeled with a compact efficiency term
inter-layer transformer dependencies are preserved only as a per-layer scheduling bound, not a full pipeline trace

required_evaluations.objective: Run a selected-point attention/KV NoC scheduler refinement using the capacity/NoC baseline transition points.

expected_result.reason: Use strict-vs-overlap scheduler bounds to pick the next detailed shared-SRAM/NoC plus producer measurement point.
